title: acalypha arvensis var. pavoniana
intro: Acalypha arvensis var. pavoniana, also known as the common weed or three-seeded mercury, is a variety of the Acalypha arvensis plant, a type of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Acalypha arvensis var. pavoniana include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, although the effectiveness and safety of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
